title: SMS Integration (Twilio)
summary: SMS integration via Twilio, covering webhook HMAC-SHA1 validation, required SMS_WEBHOOK_URL config, 1600 character message length limit, plain text only (no markdown), phone number allowlisting in E.164 format, and multi-part message handling.
key_concepts: Webhook-based message delivery (Twilio POSTs to Hermes)
SMS_WEBHOOK_URL required: public URL Twilio posts to
1600 character SMS limit with automatic splitting
Plain text only: no markdown formatting
Stateless sessions: no conversation history across SMS

env_vars.name: TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN
env_vars.description: Twilio Auth Token for HMAC-SHA1 webhook validation

env_vars.name: TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER
env_vars.description: Your Twilio phone number in E.164 format

env_vars.name: SMS_WEBHOOK_URL
env_vars.description: Public HTTPS URL where Twilio posts incoming SMS; must be set in Twilio console too

env_vars.name: SMS_ALLOWED_NUMBERS
env_vars.description: Comma-separated E.164 numbers allowed to interact

cli_commands.description: Start the messaging gateway; exposes SMS webhook endpoint

config_keys.description: Twilio Auth Token for signature validation
Your Twilio phone number in E.164 format
Public URL for Twilio to POST incoming messages to
Allowed sender phone numbers in E.164 format
Validate Twilio HMAC-SHA1 signatures (default: true, do not disable in production)

code_examples.title: SMS config
code_examples.code:
sms:
  account_sid: ""
  auth_token: ""
  phone_number: "+15551234567"
  webhook_url: "https://my-server.example.com/sms/webhook"
  allowed_numbers:
    - "+15559876543"

code_examples.title: Twilio console webhook config
code_examples.code:
# Twilio Console > Phone Numbers > Active Numbers > your number
# Messaging > A Message Comes In > Webhook
# URL: https://my-server.example.com/sms/webhook
# HTTP Method: POST

code_examples.title: HMAC-SHA1 signature validation
code_examples.code:
# Twilio signs every request with HMAC-SHA1 using your Auth Token
# Hermes validates X-Twilio-Signature header on every webhook call
# Never disable validate_signatures in production

gotchas: SMS_WEBHOOK_URL must be set in BOTH the Hermes config AND the Twilio console phone number settings
Twilio requires HTTPS for webhook URLs; self-signed certs may be rejected
SMS has a hard 1600 character limit; responses exceeding this are split into multiple messages
Plain text only: markdown formatting renders as literal symbols in SMS; Hermes strips formatting automatically
Conversation history is not maintained across separate SMS threads; each conversation is stateless unless you implement session tracking
MMS (image sending/receiving) is only supported in US and Canada on Twilio
validate_signatures: false disables the only security check; never disable in production
Twilio charges per SMS segment; long LLM responses can become expensive
full_text:
## SMS (Twilio)

Connect Hermes to SMS via [Twilio](https://www.twilio.com/).

### Setup

#### 1. Get Twilio credentials

From [console.twilio.com](https://console.twilio.com/):
- **Account SID** and **Auth Token** from the dashboard
- A **Twilio phone number** (buy one if you haven't already)

#### 2. Configure Hermes

```yaml
sms:
  account_sid: ""
  auth_token: ""
  phone_number: "+15551234567"
  webhook_url: "https://my-server.example.com/sms/webhook"
  allowed_numbers:
    - "+15559876543"
```

#### 3. Configure Twilio webhook

In the Twilio Console:
1. Go to **Phone Numbers → Active Numbers → your number**.
2. Under **Messaging → A Message Comes In**, set:
   - **Webhook URL:** 
   - **HTTP Method:** POST

### How it works

Twilio POSTs incoming SMS to your webhook URL. Hermes validates the  header using HMAC-SHA1 with your Auth Token, generates a response, and sends it back via the Twilio REST API.

### Message length

SMS supports up to 1600 characters. Responses longer than this are automatically split into multiple messages. Keeping responses concise is recommended — each SMS segment is a separate Twilio charge.

### Formatting

SMS does not support markdown. Hermes strips all formatting (bold, italic, code blocks) and sends plain text. Tables are converted to plain list format.

### MMS

Twilio supports MMS (image sending/receiving) in the US and Canada. Images sent to the Hermes number are forwarded to the vision pipeline if the active model supports it.

### Security

Hermes validates every incoming webhook request using Twilio's HMAC-SHA1 signature scheme. Never set  in production — this is the only protection against spoofed requests.

### Allowlist

```yaml
sms:
  allowed_numbers:
    - "+15551234567"
    - "+447700900123"
```

Numbers not on the allowlist receive no response (Twilio still delivers the message to the webhook; Hermes silently drops it).
